steps.title: Spot the contrast in the passage
steps.content: The word “although” signals a contrast between what the government attempted and what actually happened. The second clause—”copies of the book circulated in secret”—reveals that the government's action didn't fully succeed. This pattern suggests the government was trying to prevent something, not promote or analyze it.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the answer
steps.content: ”Suppress” means to prevent publication or distribution of something. In the sentence, the government attempted to suppress the novel, but copies still circulated in secret—this fits the meaning perfectly. The other choices don't fit the logic of a government attempting to control or prevent a publication. That matches choice C — C is correct.
